content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Verified and finalized the remaining 9ni.5.7 amendments already present in the working tree. `src/cli/project.rs` now keeps create-time same-bead blocking restricted to explicit bead-backed evidence by using the legacy default-id fallback only for recovery/adoption paths, so an unrelated manual project named like a bead default no longer blocks `project create-from-bead`. The same file also keeps all-terminal same-bead history recoverable for controller adoption by deterministically selecting a completed candidate instead of treating multiple completed matches as fatal ambiguity. `src/cli/milestone.rs` includes regression coverage proving `ensure_project_for_controller` can recover a bead after restart/controller desync when the bead has multiple completed projects. CLI coverage also proves create-from-bead ignores a task-source-free manual default-named project while preserving the duplicate-active-bead hardening.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the pending amendments against the current tree in `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/cli/milestone.rs`, and `tests/cli.rs` to confirm the two required behaviors: recoverable all-terminal same-bead history for milestone adoption and create-time exclusion of task-source-free manual default-named projects from same-bead duplicate blocking.
2. [Completed] Verified the implementation in `src/cli/project.rs` that differentiates explicit same-bead matching from the legacy default-id fallback, allowing recovery/adoption to keep legacy support while preventing create-time misclassification of unrelated manual projects.
3. [Completed] Verified the controller recovery path and regression coverage in `src/cli/milestone.rs` and `tests/cli.rs`, including deterministic selection of a completed same-bead project when all matches are terminal and successful create-from-bead behavior when only a manual default-named project exists.
4. [Completed] Ran the focused regressions and repository verification gates on the final tree, including formatting, clippy, the full locked `test-stub` suite, and the authoritative `nix build` gate.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub find_existing_bead_project_prefers_deterministic_terminal_candidate` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub ensure_project_for_controller_recovers_all_terminal_same_bead_history` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_ignores_manual_default_named_project_without_task_source` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` exited successfully.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` exited successfully.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` passed on the full tree.
- `nix build` passed on the final tree.

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
